item.title: Warwick Streamer LX 4 Jazzman - German Made 2005 - AA Curly Flamed Maple Top — $2,352.87
item.description:
$2,352.87 · Very Good · The Guitar Bazaar by Leroux Music

Leroux Music

2114A 14 st NW

Calgary


Here we have an absolutely stunning German Made Warwick Streamer LX 4 String Jazzman.


Has some rash on back side of top horn. Otherwise super clean, and flats absolutely fantastic! Plays the part.


Comes with a warwick hard rockcase at asking price.


This Warwick Streamer LX Jazz Bass is legendary and capable to suit almost any style.

Extraordinary sustain , one piece neck , bolt on for fast attack and funk styles

Beautifully flamed maple top from selected wood


Well known Wawick ergonomic body, balanced neck, frets easy accessible.


Panorama knob for bridge / neck pickup


Passive and active mode. Jazz bass single coil mode and also Music Man style bridge humbucker selectable (bridge humbucker in series and parallel mode switchable).


Technical Specs : Transparent burgundy red, gloss finish


swamp ash body with a AA curly maple top -

Bolt-on ovangkol neck with wenge fingerboard, 24 frets -

Fingerboard radius: 4-string = 500 mm (20")

Scale length: 34" ( long sale)

passive MEC J/MM pickups with open pole pieces

active MEC 3-band electronics

gold hardware - two-part Warwick bridge - Warwick tuners

Standard Security locks

Including tools, strap, bag

Weight 3,95 kg

item.title: Warwick Fortress one PJ natural【4.07kg】 — $1,239
item.description:
$1,239 · Very Good · Yamanashi Musical Instrument Store

Looking for the distinctive tone and playability that Warwick is known for? The Warwick Fortress One combines premium tonewoods, versatile electronics, and the signature Warwick sound in a highly playable 4-string bass.

The Fortress One features a maple body paired with a wenge neck and wenge fingerboard. This combination delivers the fast attack, strong midrange, and articulate low end that have become hallmarks of Warwick basses.

Its PJ pickup configuration blends the powerful punch of a Precision-style pickup with the clarity and definition of a Jazz-style bridge pickup, making the bass suitable for a wide range of musical styles.

The active electronics feature Master Volume, Balancer, Bass, and Treble controls, allowing extensive tonal shaping for stage, studio, or practice.

One of the defining features of this model is its brass frets and brass nut. These contribute to increased sustain, enhanced note definition, and a crisp attack, making the instrument equally effective for fingerstyle, pick playing, and slap techniques.

The 24-fret neck provides excellent upper-fret access, making it well suited for technical playing and solo work.

Weighing 4.07kg, the bass offers excellent balance and comfortable playability.

Cosmetically, there are a few minor scratches and dents, but the instrument remains in overall very good condition.

Specifications

Body: Maple

Neck: Wenge

Fingerboard: Wenge

Neck Joint: Bolt-On

Controls: Master Volume, Balancer, Bass, Treble

Pickup Configuration: PJ

Frets: 24 (Brass Frets)

Nut: Brass

Color: Natural

Weight: 4.07kg

Condition

Brass frets and brass nut.

Active EQ.

Minor scratches and small dents from use.

Overall very good condition.

item.title: 【new】Sadowsky / MX20 UV4 CAR/R 3.91kg #SMX A 000176-26【GIB Hyogo】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $1,449
item.description:
$1,449 · Excellent · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Sadowsky is a high-end New York brand with top-tier musicians such as Marcus Miller and Will Lee as clients. (Titles omitted)

Previously, the Metroline Express was manufactured in Japan, but as Warwick took over the distribution of Sadowsky products, manufacturing shifted to a factory in China.

While keeping the selling price down, high quality is maintained, making it a very unique product in its price range.

This is the MX20 UV4, the first PB-type guitar in the MX series.

The body is made of lightweight solid Okoume, the neck is made of Roasted Maple, and the fingerboard is made of Morado (Pau Ferro).

This is a standard combination in the MX series.

The construction has been thoroughly considered for excellent body balance and sound projection, resulting in a very refined balance that is comfortable to hold.

The back of the neck has a satin finish, providing a smooth and pleasant feel.

The pickup is a "Sadowsky passive P-Style pickup."

It has the powerful, forceful projection characteristic of PB-type basses, yet delivers a clear sound without muddy bass tones.

The preamp features the familiar 2-band EQ.

You can significantly boost the bass and treble frequencies with the touch of a button.

The circuit board of this preamp is almost identical to that of the top-of-the-line models priced around 1 million yen.

Copper foil shielding is also applied, resulting in surprisingly low noise.

These points are extremely significant advantages when choosing an MX series bass.

Even in this price range, Roger Sadowsky's uncompromising commitment to instrument making is evident.

While offering the ultra-smooth playability characteristic of Sadowsky, the sound is unmistakably that of a P-bass.

It outputs a modern tone that beautifully blends a robust punch with sweet, airy, and clear treble tones.

Based on the traditional PB design, this guitar incorporates modern elements, embodying Roger Sadowsky's philosophy.

SPECS

Scale Length: 34" (864 mm) Long Scale
Body Shape: P-Style body
Body Wood: Okoume, Solidbody
Neck Wood: Roasted Maple (C-Shape, Bolt-On)
Fingerboard Wood: Morado
Inlays: White dot inlays
Fingerboard Radius: 12"
Nut: Sadowsky Just-A-Nut III
Nut Width: 1.5" (38.5 mm)
Nut String Spacing: Nut string spacing (all strings): 1.22" / 31 mm
Neck width at 12th fret: 2.22" / 56.3 mm neck width at 12th fret
Neck width at last fret: 2.59" / 65.7 mm neck width at last fret
Neck thickness at 1st fret: 0.82" / 20.8 mm neck thickness at 1st fret
Neck thickness at 12th fret: 0.96" / 24.6 mm neck thickness at 12th fret
# Frets: 20 Nickel Silver (width: 0.094" (2.4 mm) / height: 0.051" (1.3 mm))
Neck Pickup: Sadowsky passive P-Style pickup
Bridge Pickup: None
Preamp: Active Sadowsky 2-way electronics (Bass and Treble boost)
Controls: Volume / Treble / Bass
Push/Pull Function: Push/pull function at Bass knob for preamp bypass
Machine Heads: Sadowsky Light Open Gear Machine Heads
Security Locks: Sadowsky S-Security Locks
Bridge: Sadowsky bridge with quick string release
Bridge Total String Spacing: 2.24" / 57mm
Hardware Color: Chrome
Strings: Sadowsky Silver Label, Stainless Steel, 045 - 105
Protector: Sadowsky Fretboard Protector
Tools: Hex key tools
Bag / Case: Sadowsky PortaBag
Made In: China

Accessories: Original gig bag, warranty card

Text: Kaigo Kawami

Product photos: Muragishi

Manufacturer's warranty included

(Warranty is only valid for purchases made in Japan)

●Price includes VAT & Duties & Shipping Fee
Free Shipping Worldwide. All import duties and taxes are included in the price. There are no hidden fees upon delivery.

If you return a product, you will be responsible for all shipping and customs fees.

Note: All electronic devices, such as effect pedals and amplifiers, are set for use in Japan.
Please be sure to check the voltage and power specifications of your country before purchasing and using.

item.title: 1981 Fender Precision Bass - ex The Fall with Mark E Smith — $2,964.27
item.description:
$2,964.27 · Excellent · Steve's Gear Emporium

1981 Fender Precision bass in excellent condition. Very clean, with only a few marks and one noticeable ding on the top side. Pickups were replaced long ago with Seymour Duncan Quarter Pounders. There are screw holes next to the bridge where a different bridge was once fitted by the previous owner. This bass was owned and used by Steve Trafford from The Fall and was used to record the 'Fall Heads Roll' album and used as his main instrument on tour and in the studio. This is the bass that was used to record 'Blindness'. It was later used by Steve Hanley from The Fall when he played in Brix & the Extricated. It is a fantastic sounding and playing instrument that has been well cared for. There is some footage below from The Falls appearance on Later with Jools Holland that features the bass. Comes with a hard Hiscox case

item.title: FUJIGEN(FGN) JMP2B-ASH-M -Open Pore Black- #G260021[3.71kg][Made In Japan] — $1,083.52
item.description:
$1,083.52 · Brand New · Kurosawa Music Ikebukuro

-----

*Caution!!

To prevent fraudulent purchases, we do not accept PayPal guest checkout.

Please create your PayPal account before make the payment.

-----

Welcome to

Kurosawa Ikebukuro! We can ship anywhere all over the world!!

Thank you for looking! Feel free to ask for more details. Also, please carefully read our

shop policy below the guitar description!

For Paypal payments, please register your address in your Paypal account in advance.

------------------------------------------

◆＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿ ◆


Among the many FUJIGEN guitars in stock, this one stands out! !Just look at this look.

First of all, the bold specification of "two P-type pickups" is what immediately catches the eye.

In fact, this is a very popular model, receiving constant requests from players both domestically and internationally who say, "I've been waiting for this specification!"


Its reputation has spread in Japan, and the number of people purchasing it has increased, so now I, the person in charge, can confidently recommend this model.

And what's more, this isn't just any model; it's a [limited production item]!

Incidentally, the blue model is already sold out, and we really don't know when this model will also become unavailable...


A limited edition specification packed with playfulness and attention to detail not found in the regular line.

Before you regret not buying it then, be sure to check it out!


◆＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿◆


The Craftsmanship Shines Through in the "Open Pore Satin Finish"

I hope this texture comes across in the images...!


This finish utilizes the natural irregularities of the wood, leaving the wood grain unfilled.

It's characterized by the direct texture unique to ash, and the powerful grain of the ash stands out against the chic matte black...

Because this finish leaves no room for error, it embodies the skills of Fujigen's proud, highly skilled craftsmen.


The headstock, finished in the same matte black as the body, looks fantastic and really tightens up the overall look.

This is a guitar that exudes "mature charm" and is sure to look great on stage!


◆＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿◆


【Sound】

A surprisingly "thick and versatile" tone that belies its appearance!

With this look, you might think it has a "strong character," but it actually has an incredibly "usable" sound!


It's luxuriously equipped with two Seymour Duncan split-coil pickups.

While retaining the "softness," "thickness," and "elasticity" characteristic of the Precision Bass, it has evolved into a more powerful and dense sound.

The best way to describe it is "a Jazz Bass sound, but thicker and more robust."

Depending on the tone and volume adjustments and playing technique, you can even get a surprisingly natural Precision Bass sound.


And it pairs perfectly with distortion!


It's designed with heavy, pick-played metal and hardcore in mind.


Even when tuned down to Drop D, the sound doesn't become muddy, and the sheer power of the "wall of sound" when distortion is applied is breathtaking!


【Playability】

It features a stress-free, aggressive design.


The back of the neck has a smooth, matte (satin) finish.


It doesn't get sticky even when you sweat, and position changes are smooth!


This is an irresistible feature for players who frequently use fast playing and melodic phrases.


The nut is 40mm wide, slightly narrower than a typical Precision Bass.


The shape has a comfortable thickness, yet it fits comfortably even for those with small hands or female players.


You can grip it without putting unnecessary force on your thumb, making it less tiring even during long playing sessions.


And it's easy to reach the high positions!


The body joint is rounded, allowing for smooth access to the high frets. Furthermore, the contoured body perfectly matches the contours of your body, providing an incredible sense of unity when you hold it!


The satin finish on the body makes it easy to play, even with techniques that involve a lot of right-hand movement—a unique feature of this model.


"I like the sound of a Precision Bass, but I want a little more power."


"I want the playability of a Jazz Bass but with a thicker sound."... This model fulfills those ambitious desires.


◆＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿◆


【Specifications】

1. Wood Composition

Ash body, maple fingerboard, maple neck


2. Electronics

Traditional 2 volume, 1 tone

Equipped with Seymour Duncan Quarter-Pound SPB-3 pickups.


3. Hardware

Features a string-through-body bridge.

Down tuning is also considered, allowing you to choose your preferred stringing method.


4. Circle Fretting System (C.F.S.)

Fujigen's unique patented technology.

By installing the frets in an arc shape, each string and fret intersect at a right angle, achieving accurate pitch and rich sustain.

The clear sound with less muddiness in chords is a great asset in ensembles.


5. Finish

Open-pore finish uses urethane.

Many people have the image of a thick, glossy finish, but as with this model, "urethane that is deliberately applied thinly by craftsmen, preserving the wood's natural texture" is actually a very time-consuming and luxurious finish.


◆＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿◆


【Accessories】

Soft case, hex wrench


【SPEC】

Body: Ash

Neck: Maple U-Shape

Fingerboard: Maple 21F Medium Jumbo C.F.S.

Scale: 863.6 mm / 34 in

Bridge: FGBB-104 (String Pitch = 19mm)

Tuning: GOTOH GB11W

Control: 2 Volume, 1 Tone

Pickup: Seymour Duncan Quarter Pound SPB-3

item.title: Ibanez EHB1500 - Cosmic Blue Starburst Flat - [2.89kg][#I250908610] — $1,277
item.description:
$1,277 · Brand New · Kurosawa Music Ochanomizu

*Caution!!

To prevent fraudulent purchases, we do not accept PayPal guest checkout.

Please create your PayPal account before make the payment.

As clearly stated in our policy, we cannot accept returns or refunds for customer-related reasons.

This includes situations such as high customs duties, shipping delays, product colors differing from expectations, items being heavy, difficult to play ability.



“Ibanez” has released the “Ultimate Ergonomic Headless Bass.”


■Ergonomic Body Design


The EHB body design is based on ergonomics to ensure the instrument fits the player perfectly, whether playing standing or seated.

The horn is shaped to balance the body and neck while standing and to provide a comfortable grip, while the body curve on the high-string side is designed with a particular emphasis on fit and balance when playing seated.

The area of the body surface where the elbow rests features a contoured slope. Additionally, all body edges that come into contact with the body are finished with smooth rounded edges.


■Slanted Body Back


The back of the body features a bold cut that tapers toward the low strings. This design enhances the fit during seated playing and takes into account the angle of the player’s line of sight when viewing the fingerboard inlays.

Additionally, to facilitate access to the high-fret areas, the back of the horn on the treble side is cut in a scooped shape.


■Chambered Body

The body features chambering to allow for a more visceral sense of its resonance.

By directly feeling the vibrations inside the body—much like a hollow-body guitar—the player can better recognize the sound they are producing.

This also results in a slight reduction in weight.


■9-Piece Panga Panga/Walnut Neck with Graphite Reinforcement Rods

We’ve adopted a 9-piece neck combining Panga Panga and walnut.

Both are hardwoods, making it easy to achieve a clear sound image and characterized by a crisp, well-defined tone.


■Panga Panga Fretboard & Medium Stainless Steel Frets

We’ve adopted a Panga Panga fretboard, which, due to the hardness of the wood, adds a clear core to the sound image.


■Nordstrand? Custom Big Split Pickup

This model is equipped with a Nordstrand® Big Split custom pickup.

This split pickup delivers a rich low end with a warm tone. It naturally covers everything from sounds ideal for bass lines to funky slap sounds.


■Vari-Mid 3-Band EQ w/EQ bypass switch

As the abbreviation for “Variable frequency control for Mid EQ” suggests, this EQ offers flexible control over the midrange.

Typically, a 3-band EQ divides the frequency range into three bands—HIGH, MID, and LOW—and allows the user to boost or cut specific frequencies preset by the manufacturer. However, with the Vari-Mid, the user can freely adjust the focus of the midrange.


■MR5HS Mono Rail Bridge with Adjustable String Spacing Saddles

This model features a newly developed, proprietary headless-specific bridge.

Tuning is performed by turning the bridge end. While the stiffness of this turning mechanism has been a weakness in other manufacturers’ products, we’ve achieved smooth operation by combining our unique design with Teflon washers.

The saddles can be moved 1.5 mm to the left or right, allowing you to adjust the spacing between strings (string pitch) as desired within the range of motion. This enables fine-tuning to suit your playing style.


･Soft case for electric guitar included

“Ibanez” has released the “Ultimate Ergonomic Headless Bass.”


■Ergonomic Body Design


The EHB body design is based on ergonomics to ensure the instrument fits the player perfectly, whether playing standing or seated.

The horn is shaped to balance the body and neck while standing and to provide a comfortable grip, while the body curve on the high-string side is designed with a particular emphasis on fit and balance when playing seated.

The area of the body surface where the elbow rests features a contoured slope. Additionally, all body edges that come into contact with the body are finished with smooth rounded edges.


■Slanted Body Back


The back of the body features a bold cut that tapers toward the low strings. This design enhances the fit during seated playing and takes into account the angle of the player’s line of sight when viewing the fingerboard inlays.

Additionally, to facilitate access to the high-fret areas, the back of the horn on the treble side is cut in a scooped shape.


■Chambered Body

The body features chambering to allow for a more visceral sense of its resonance.

By directly feeling the vibrations inside the body—much like a hollow-body guitar—the player can better recognize the sound they are producing.

This also results in a slight reduction in weight.


■9-Piece Panga Panga/Walnut Neck with Graphite Reinforcement Rods

We’ve adopted a 9-piece neck combining Panga Panga and walnut.

Both are hardwoods, making it easy to achieve a clear sound image and characterized by a crisp, well-defined tone.


■Panga Panga Fretboard & Medium Stainless Steel Frets

We’ve adopted a Panga Panga fretboard, which, due to the hardness of the wood, adds a clear core to the sound image.


■Nordstrand? Custom Big Split Pickup

This model is equipped with a Nordstrand® Big Split custom pickup.

This split pickup delivers a rich low end with a warm tone. It naturally covers everything from sounds ideal for bass lines to funky slap sounds.


■Vari-Mid 3-Band EQ w/EQ bypass switch

As the abbreviation for “Variable frequency control for Mid EQ” suggests, this EQ offers flexible control over the midrange.

Typically, a 3-band EQ divides the frequency range into three bands—HIGH, MID, and LOW—and allows the user to boost or cut specific frequencies preset by the manufacturer. However, with the Vari-Mid, the user can freely adjust the focus of the midrange.


■MR5HS Mono Rail Bridge with Adjustable String Spacing Saddles

This model features a newly developed, proprietary headless-specific bridge.

Tuning is performed by turning the bridge end. While the stiffness of this turning mechanism has been a weakness in other manufacturers’ products, we’ve achieved smooth operation by combining our unique design with Teflon washers.

The saddles can be moved 1.5 mm to the left or right, allowing you to adjust the spacing between strings (string pitch) as desired within the range of motion. This enables fine-tuning to suit your playing style.


･Soft case for electric guitar included

This item is being offered at a special price due to display scratches.


The “ultimate ergonomic headless bass” from “Ibanez” has arrived at our outlet.


When you actually hold it, you’ll be surprised by how incredibly light it is and how exceptionally easy it is to handle.

While the “SR Series” from “Ibanez” is renowned for its light weight and wide range of sound-shaping capabilities,

this bass surpasses even those models with its superior playability, ease of handling, and even broader range of sound-shaping options.


One feature worth noting is the “continuously variable mid-frequency control,” which was previously featured on models like the “K-5.”

While the “SR Series” primarily featured “3-step variable” controls, the adoption of “continuously variable mid-frequency control”

allows for precise boosting and cutting of the midrange—the “heart” of the bass’s sound—

enabling deeper sound shaping.

As a result, I believe it can adapt to a wide variety of playing styles.


Additionally, thanks to its “chambered construction,”

it not only helps keep the instrument lightweight,

but also provides a rich, resonant body tone,

making this bass highly recommended even for those who typically dislike the harsh sound characteristic of headless models.


The body is crafted from “African Mahogany,” while the top features “Poplar Burl.”

Compared to the “EHB1000,” it stands out for its luxurious appearance and rich mid-low frequencies,

producing a warm, sweet tone that’s truly pleasing to the ear.


It is equipped with a “Nordstrand” “Custom Big Split pickup.”

This combination capitalizes on the characteristics of “African Mahogany”

while adding a sharp attack.


The neck features a 9-piece construction of “Pangapanga” and “Walnut.”

This design offers even greater strength than the “EHB1000,”

making it resistant to warping and twisting,

resulting in a robust neck.


The frets are made of “Stainless Steel,”

which preserves the sharp attack while also delivering impressive durability—a welcome feature at this price point.


This is the “ultimate ergonomic headless bass,”

with a meticulous attention to playability evident in every detail.


We hope you’ll take this opportunity to consider it.



■SPEC
Neck : EHB5 9pc Pangapanga／Walnut neck w／Graphite reinforcement rods
Body :Poplar Burl Top ／ African Mahogany body
Fretboard : Bound Panga Panga fretboard w／Abalone off-set dot inlay
Fret : Medium Stainless Steel
Bridge : MR5HS bridge (18mm string spacing)
Pickup : Nordstrand Custom Big Split pickup
EQ : Vari-mid 3-band EQ w/EQ bypass switch (passive tone control on treble pot)
Machine Head : Ibanez custom headpieces

item.description:
$3,018.69 · Brand New · Ikebe Music

Our Business Hours is Mon to Fri / 11:00 to 18:00 JPN time
We will close Sat and Sun, so we will reply to inquiry that received weekend on next week.
Your understanding will be appreciated.

So occasionally it will be sold out while having email communication.
Please understand in advance.

Japanese consumption tax is not required for international shopping. However you may be required to pay duty, tax and import fee when receiving your items in your country. You must pay all these duty, tax and fee as receiver/importer and we do not cover any of them.

■Please note
The product description is an automatic translation of the Japanese description. Please contact us if you have any questions.

-----------------------------------

[Atelier Z × IKEBE Original Pouch Gift]
We're giving away an original pouch (not for sale) created through a collaboration between Atelier Z and IKEBE!
It fits in the pocket of your gig bag and is convenient for storing maintenance tools like wrenches that tend to get scattered around!
This is a rare item that can only be obtained by those who purchase an Atelier Z bass guitar at Ikebe. Take this opportunity to find your dream Atelier Z!

Eligible products: Purchase an ATELIER Z bass guitar priced at 300,000 yen or more (including tax) and receive a free gift. *This is a limited-quantity gift, so please note that it will be discontinued once supplies run out.
*Some items, such as outlet items, sale items, reused/second-hand items, and made-to-order items, may be excluded.
----------------------------------------

This is a color-ordered version of the upgraded 24-fret Beta 5-string model.

ATELIER Z Guitar Works enjoys the deep trust of professional bassists of all musical styles. Among the brand's extensive lineup of basses, characterized by a clear sound orientation and a variety of specifications to meet the needs of players, this particular model is the "Beta," which features a 24-fret design and a deep cutaway on the treble side for excellent playability in the higher positions. It is equipped with an ash body, a celluloid-wrapped maple neck, and an original bridge. While retaining the tight and powerful bass tones that are characteristic of the brand's flagship model "M (Magnitude)," this model also features a sharp body shape, adding a crisp and well-defined nuance to its sound.

One of the great things about ATELIER Z basses is that they deliver a solid, resonant acoustic sound even with a low string action. Not only is the sound crisp when slapping, but you also get a well-defined bottom end when fingerpicking or using a pick. The thick pickguard and the exquisite Z-tuning system create a tight string spacing, allowing for supple responsiveness to fretting and picking nuances, contributing to stable playing and highlighting its excellence as a tool to support the player's expression. You can fully enjoy the slap sound that has become synonymous with this brand. Furthermore, even when boosting the sound on the bass itself during slapping, there's no loss of clarity or fragmentation of the sound image; instead, it significantly enhances the bass's presence. In addition to the brand's signature crisp sound, the 24-fret design offers nimble playability and ease of handling technical phrases, which are key to this model's popularity. It's also recommended for those who want to play various genres with a single bass.

The Beta series, which had been in the lineup for a long time, underwent a renewal in March 2025, and along with that, the models and specifications were also refreshed. The string spacing is now 19mm as standard, and the pickups are the company's original "JZ-5" pickups, which employ a slant pole piece/split coil structure, achieving a noiseless and powerful sound. Furthermore, it is equipped with the newly developed "AZ-3B" active preamp. Each of the 3 bands can be boosted/cut, covering a wide range of sounds. In addition, a heel cut at the joint and a removable finger ramp have been included, actively incorporating features for technically-oriented players. Be sure to get your hands on one of the two flagship models, which has been further enhanced in every aspect and has become even more powerful.

【Specifications】
Scale: 34 inches
Neck: Maple
Fingerboard: Maple 24F
Body: Ash2P
Tuning key: GOTOH GB-528
Bridge: ATELIER Z BB519
Pickups: ATELIER Z JZ-5
Preamp: ATELIER Z AZ-3B (Treble: ±18dB/7KHz, Middle: ±7dB/400Hz, Bass: ±8dB/70Hz, Gain: with trimmer)
Controls: M'Vol., Balancer, M'Tone/sw, MID, Treble/Bass
Weight: ≈4.19kg

Gig bag included

This model, the SR300E series, is an entry-level model that incorporates the features and design of the SR series.
Featuring original pickups known for their crisp, clear sound, an original active preamp with 3-band boost and cut capabilities, and a tap switch with three selectable coil patterns, this bass guitar allows for a wide range of sound shaping. With a comfortable, smooth-feeling 5-piece maple neck and an Accu-cast bridge that prevents lateral saddle movement, every detail is meticulously crafted, making it a safe and reliable choice even for those just starting out on bass.

Atelier Z, a professional brand that Japan proudly presents to the world. Backed by over 30 years of experience and a proven track record, the “Z Tuning System” delivers comfortable playability and retains the dazzling, distinctive tone that defined an era. Yet, rather than conforming to the now-conventional image of a one-dimensional “bright and punchy” sound, its solid, fundamental sound is fully realized even in passive mode. The brand’s original philosophy of crafting all-around instruments continues to evolve to this day.


This custom model features Bartolini XTCT and MCT-375 preamps and is finished in See-Through Blue.

With the midrange control exposed on the front panel, you can now fine-tune your sound with even greater precision. This makes the M Series—one of Atelier Z’s most popular lines—even easier to play and delivers a more modern sound.


Don’t miss this opportunity.


■SPEC

Atelier Z, a professional brand that Japan proudly presents to the world. Backed by over 30 years of experience and a proven track record, the “Z Tuning System” delivers comfortable playability and retains the dazzling, distinctive tone that defined an era. Yet, rather than conforming to the now-conventional image of a one-dimensional “bright and punchy” sound, its solid, fundamental sound is fully realized even in passive mode. The brand’s original philosophy of crafting all-around instruments continues to evolve to this day.


This time, it was ordered in a matte white finish.

The simple color pairs perfectly with the acrylic pickguard and control panel unique to Atelier Z!


Additionally, by adding the MCT375—a staple in our custom orders—active mid-range control is now possible directly on the body surface.

The controls consist of master volume and tone controls, a pickup balancer, mid-range, treble/bass controls, and an active/passive switch.


This model was ordered in a matte white finish.

With its transparent pickguard, it has a simple yet distinctive look.



■SPEC

Body: Ash 2P

Neck: Maple 1P

Bridge: Atelier Z BB419 (19mm)

item.title: Vanzandt PBV-R2 - 2-Tone Sunburst - [3.96kg] — $1,851.65
item.description:
$1,851.65 · Brand New · Kurosawa Music Ochanomizu

*Caution!!

To prevent fraudulent purchases, we do not accept PayPal guest checkout.

Please create your PayPal account before make the payment.



Vanzandt is a long-established Japanese brand.

While many other manufacturers’ passive models incorporate unique elements (such as a 21-fret design with an extended nut) to differentiate themselves, this brand produces models crafted in an extremely orthodox style.


In a good way, the fact that there are few standout features speaks to the brand’s commitment to vintage-inspired, classic specifications, with unnecessary elements eliminated as much as possible.

If there’s one specification that truly defines this brand, it would be the use of an ultra-thin all-lacquer finish.

Most vintage basses feature lacquer finishes, but due to cost and technical constraints, it’s difficult to mass-produce lacquer-finished basses today.


Among electric basses in the same price range, thicker polyurethane finishes are commonly used. While polyurethane—known for its hardness—has the advantage of maintaining a beautiful appearance for a long time, it tends to suppress the wood’s natural resonance, resulting in a tight sound—especially when the finish is thick.


In contrast, a lacquer finish is soft and brittle, developing a dignified, vintage-like appearance with prolonged use.

Furthermore, because the finish is softer, the wood and finish resonate more easily together, providing an open and direct tone.

Since this model features an ultra-thin finish, this effect is particularly pronounced.

With instruments featuring this type of finish, the more you play, the more the wood’s resonance becomes unique to you—and isn’t it a joy to be able to grow alongside your instrument?


As mentioned above, while this model is inspired by vintage specifications, its greatest appeal lies in the fact that it goes beyond a simple homage to vintage designs and possesses boundless potential.


In terms of sound, there’s absolutely nothing to complain about.

It offers a wide-ranging sound with clear definition, yet within that, you can distinctly sense a rich, refined texture.

Although it is, after all, a Japanese-made instrument, it gives the impression of having a strong American influence.


While many Japanese brands are releasing vintage-style instruments, there aren’t many models that come this close to the ideal.

Don’t miss this opportunity.


BODY: ALDER

NECK: INDIAN ROSEWOOD ON MAPLE / 20F

NECK SCALE 34” (864mm)

RADIUS 210R

NUT OIL-FINISHED BONE / 40.5mm

PICKUPS P-STYLE BASS

FINISH ALL NITROCELLULOSE LACQUER


Comes with gig case

item.title: Nash Guitars JB63 - Black - [4.20kg] — $2,115.44
item.description:
$2,115.44 · Very Good · Kurosawa Music Ochanomizu

*Caution!!

To prevent fraudulent purchases, we do not accept PayPal guest checkout.

Please create your PayPal account before make the payment.




Nash Guitars is a pioneering brand in aged—or rather, relic—finishes.

The main components are handcrafted in the United States, and thanks to their exceptional quality, these guitars are favored by numerous artists and engineers and have earned high acclaim.

While various brands have been releasing high-quality aged models in recent years, Nash Guitars continues to attract significant attention.

This brand pays meticulous attention not only to the exterior but also to the interior, going beyond simply replicating the appearance of vintage instruments to recreate that vintage-like feel in the sound as faithfully as possible.


The “63” model typically features an alder body and rosewood fingerboard as standard specifications.

That said, it’s a model where options such as an ash body or maple fingerboard are available; this particular instrument features a maple fingerboard with a black, smoky silver finish.


One of NASH’s defining characteristics is its excellent resonance, likely due to the combination of carefully selected woods and a thin finish.

Furthermore, as you play it, you’ll sense that it has the potential to develop an even richer, more mature tone over time.


The frets show almost no wear—about 90% remaining—and there is plenty of adjustment range left on the truss rod.

There are currently no issues with the electronics, so it’s ready to play right out of the box.


Don’t miss out.


Included: Hard case

item.title: 【used】Stem / Ove4 3.885kg #stem0159【GIB Yokohama】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $1,741
item.description:
$1,741 · Very Good · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Stem is a brand produced by Sago New Material Guitars, a handmade brand from the Kansai region of Japan.

It's positioned between Sago, which specializes in custom-order production, and Seed, which achieves high quality through excellent quality control while keeping costs down by manufacturing overseas.

It's known for producing models that retain Sago's high quality while keeping costs down by fixing specifications and shapes.

This model is the Stem version of Sago's flagship model, the Ove.

The body is made of alder, and the fingerboard is rosewood, a classic wood combination.

The color is a multi-layer finish with a white base, layered with vibrant yellow and turquoise blue.

The high quality of the paintwork, typical of Sago, is clearly evident.

The pickups are original L(x)LJB.

These are standard pickups also used in higher-end models.

The controls are simple passive.

It features a series/parallel switch for the pickups.

The bridge and tuning machines are made by GOTOH, showing no compromise on hardware. The sound has a unique Sago-esque impactful low end.

While possessing a modern, wide range, it also features a well-crafted, rich midrange, making it perfectly suited to ensembles.

Fret life is approximately 80%.

The truss rod is in good condition.

The neck is in good condition.

The body and paintwork are clean overall, with no noticeable scratches or stains.

The electronics are in good condition.

No obvious modifications were observed.

Controls: Master Volume, Balancer, Master Tone, Pickup Wiring Switch
Nut Width: 38.0mm
Bridge String Spacing: 19.0mm
Scale Length: 34 Inch
Year of Manufacture: Unknown
Country of Manufacture: Japan

During setup, we used our original GIB BASIC / Standard Bass Strings.

These strings are characterized by their straightforward sound and stable playability, naturally bringing out the instrument's inherent character. These are highly reliable strings that we actually use in our repair and setup workshops.

Please enjoy these strings, which aim to be a new standard for bassists.

They are delivered after being adjusted for easy playability by GIB's craftsmen.

Accessories: Original gig bag, wrench

Text: Tomoki Hayakawa

Inspection 담당: Oshima

Product photos: Higashie

GIB 3-month warranty included

◯GIB, TONIQ Warranty Information

●Lifetime Free Maintenance

Instruments purchased from our store are eligible for lifetime free "String Vibration Setup" (normally ¥4,400). (Reservation required)

・Please present your warranty card with your name filled in.

・Only applicable to the person who purchased the instrument at a GIB or TONIQ physical store or online shop.

・Please bear the cost of shipping and other miscellaneous expenses.

●Re-buyback Price Guarantee

If you sell your purchased item within 6 months of purchase, we guarantee the buyback price. The price is 50% for new items and 70% for used items.

●Warranty against defects

Basic Co., Ltd. provides a warranty against defects for products sold by the company. Excluding some items such as consigned goods, we will repair (or refund if repair is not possible) any malfunctions that occur during normal use within a certain warranty period from the date of purchase free of charge.

The warranty period is 3 months for used items and 1 year for new items.

Please note that the following cases will incur a charge even within the warranty period:

* Malfunctions and damage caused by transportation, movement, dropping, etc.

* Replacement of consumables.

* Malfunctions and damage caused by fire, earthquake, salt damage, gas damage, or abnormal voltage.

* Malfunctions and damage caused by handling contrary to the precautions described in the instruction manual.

* Malfunctions or damage to parts repaired or modified by a party other than our company.

* If the warranty card does not have your name, model name, serial number, etc. filled in, or if the wording has been altered.

* Failure to present the warranty card, or if it is lost.

(Warranty is only valid for purchases made within Japan.)

● Up to 15% OFF with Trade-in

When you purchase a new instrument, you will receive a 10% discount on the purchase price when you use our trade-in service. (Consignment items are excluded. This discount applies only if the trade-in value is 15% or more of the purchase price.)

Furthermore, if you trade in an instrument eligible for our monthly buyback campaign, the discount rate will be 15%.

We ship with sufficient packaging.

We also ship internationally.

●Price includes VAT & Duties & Shipping Fee
Free Shipping Worldwide. All import duties and taxes are included in the price. There are no hidden fees upon delivery.

If you return a product, you will be responsible for all shipping and customs fees.

Note: All electronic devices, such as effect pedals and amplifiers, are set for use in Japan.
Please be sure to check the voltage and power specifications of your country before purchasing and using.

item.description:

Fender is, of course, the number one brand of electric guitars and basses.

It's loved by countless top artists, including James Jamerson, Bobby Vega, and Michael League. (Titles omitted)

Squier, a brand under the Fender umbrella, specializes in lower-priced products.

It offers many unique models not found in Fender's lineup, attracting the attention of many guitar fans.

While Squier is often perceived as a budget brand, its Vintage Modified and Classic Vibe series, in particular, are frequently praised for their quality.

This is the Mustang Bass from the Classic Vibe series.

The body is made of Nyatoh, the neck is Maple, and the fretboard is Indian Laurel.

The pickups are Fender Designed Alnico Split Single-Coil.

It uses a 30-inch short scale.

While some might worry about a lack of bass, in reality, the string amplitude is increased compared to a long scale, resulting in a fat and distinctive sound. This guitar possesses a captivating charm that makes you want to keep playing it, thanks to its manageable size.

The frets have approximately 90% remaining.

The truss rod is in good condition.

The neck is in good condition.

The body and paintwork have some minor scratches, but overall it is clean.

item.title: Rickenbacker 4001 C64 Mod. - Firegro "Psychedelic Paint - [4.50Kg] — $5,146.71
item.description:
$5,146.71 · Very Good · Kurosawa Music Ochanomizu

*Caution!!

To prevent fraudulent purchases, we do not accept PayPal guest checkout.

Please create your PayPal account before make the payment.




A must-have for fans has arrived in our used inventory.




Rickenbacker—a brand that has always been revered as a one-of-a-kind icon, admired across generations.


This instrument is a modified version of the 2008 4001 C64 - Fireglo.




Paul McCartney used this distinctive-looking guitar for only a short time; it was a 4001 that Rickenbacker had gifted him, which he then painted himself.


Since the paint was later stripped away, revealing the natural wood finish, no original examples survive—it is preserved only in numerous videos and photographs.


We had no choice but to analyze and study these records to recreate it.




It masterfully recreates that distinctive look—not only in color but also in meticulous details like the chip in the pickguard near the screw on the rear volume knob—resulting in a truly exquisite finish.




There is some slight tarnish on the metal parts and some weather checking in the finish in places, but considering its age, this is to be expected, and it can still be considered in excellent condition.




While many examples have neck issues, this particular instrument currently measures approximately 3 mm on the 4th string side and 2.4 mm on the 1st string side.


For a Rickenbacker product, this is well within an acceptable range.




The C Series is already extremely rare.


And this is a top-tier piece, finished with the utmost attention to detail and perfection.




Don’t miss out.




Weight: Approx. 4.50 kg


Included: Original hard case

item.description:
$2,164 · Very Good · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Vanzandt manufactures high-quality guitars and basses, and is particularly renowned for the quality of its vintage-style production.

This is a traditional 4-string JB model.

The body is made of alder, and the fingerboard is Indian rosewood.

This is a classic combination that includes a rich midrange.

The pickups are Vanzandt originals.

The pots are CTS, and the jack is Switchcraft – a perfect choice.

The sound is characteristic of a JB, featuring powerful lows and percussive highs.

Yet, it also has a refined, Japanese-made feel, possessing a charm that goes beyond a simple copy.

This is a superb instrument that we recommend trying for anyone looking for a high-quality Japanese-made 4-string JB.

The frets have approximately 70% remaining.

The truss rod is in good condition.

The neck is in good condition.

There are several dents of about 5-10mm on the back of the body and paintwork. Scratches are visible throughout the body. There are three areas on the body top where screw holes have been filled.

The electronics are in good condition.

The tone control was malfunctioning, so we replaced the potentiometer and capacitor.

Controls: Neck pickup volume, bridge pickup volume, master tone
Nut width: 38.3mm
Bridge string spacing: 20.0mm
Scale: 34 inches

Fender is, of course, the number one brand of electric guitars and basses.

It's loved by countless top artists, including Jaco Pastorious, Marcus Miller, and Geddy Lee. (Titles omitted)

This model is a Mustang Bass from the cost-effective Mexican-made Player series.

The body is made of alder, the neck is maple, and the fingerboard is pau ferro.

The neck pickup is a Vintage-Style Split Single-Coil Precision Bass, and the bridge pickup is a Vintage-Style Single-Coil Jazz Bass.

It features a 30-inch short scale.

While some might worry about a lack of bass, in reality, the string amplitude is greater than with a long scale, resulting in a fat and distinctive sound.

Combined with its easy-to-handle size, this is a guitar that you'll want to keep playing.

The frets have approximately 80% remaining.

The truss rod is in good condition.

The neck is in good condition. The nut grooves are slightly low, causing slight interference with the first fret when playing open strings, but this is not noticeable when amplified.

The body and paintwork show minor dents and scratches throughout. There are also scratches on the pickguard, likely from picking.

The electronics are in good condition.

No obvious modifications were observed.

item.title: Rickenbacker 4001 Burgundyglo 1973 Bass Guitar [Used] — $4,298
item.description:
$4,298 · Good · Bella Romeo Music

Rickenbacker is a USA based instrument manufacturer, and their instruments are beloved by famous acts around the world, such as the Beatles, Deep Purple and Pink Floyd. Here at Bella Romeo music, we present a vintage 1973 Rickenbacker 4001 in Burgundyglo. This bass guitar has a maple body wood construction, which offers bright tonal character and crisp attack. It features a 20-fret rosewood fingerboard and 3-piece walnut/ maple neck-through neck. The headstock has the walnut wing feature which is synonymous with Rickenbacker instruments.  The rosewood gives the instrument warmth and classy aesthetics, and the 3 piece walnut/ maple neck gives a combination of tight low ends and brightness, and the neck-through feature gives the bass more sustain as well. For electronics, it is equipped with the Rickenbacker toaster single coil in the neck position and Rickenbacker single coil in the bridge position, which gives the classic Rickenbacker sound that so many musicians adored.

This bass is used and it is in good condition. Since this bass is a vintage instrument, there are visible wears all around the body, but there are nothing that looks out of place and it retains an elegant look. There are some cracks on the original pickguard. There are also signs of usage on the fingerboard with several spots of finish wears on the fingerboard and on the edge of the inlays, however the bass still plays beautifully. There are wears on the headstock. There are about 50-60% of the frets remaining. The electronics are working normally, the chrome finish remains with some oxidation. The screws are rusty but they are all original. There are significant wear on the bridge mutes, and the A and D strings are functioning as intended, but the mute on the E and G string has worn too low. This bass comes with an original deluxe hardshell case, which is severely worn due to its age, and one of the buckles is non-functional.

This item is listed on Reverb.com as well as being sold Bellaromeo online store and our physical showroom in Wan Chai, Hong Kong.  In rare occasions, the item may be sold out first via other channels, in which case we regrettably will need to prioritise the order fulfilment by the time stamp of the order being placed on a first-come-first-sold basis.  You may find  additional images and item specifications on Bellaromeo online store.

item.title: Freedom Custom Guitar Research Rhino 5ST 2021 [Used] — $4,644
item.description:
$4,644 · Mint · Bella Romeo Music

Freedom Custom Guitar Research are based in Japan, and they make the highest quality of instruments. Their craftsman are passionate about their products and they focus on bringing quality sound, feel, looks and consistency to their instrument, which those philosophy are shown in the Rhino 5ST that we currently have in stock. This bass features a maple top and 2-piece alder body wood construction, which the maple gives the bass a stunning look and some snap, while the alder provides some balance. One of the unique features of this bass is the patented ARIMIZO and one point joint, which gives the bass more control to the tone. The bolt of the joint can be tightened or loosened, and it can vary from being more snappy and bright to a warmer sound with sweet overtones. This gives the bass unrivalled versatility and it can certainly handle any types of genres and tones. It features a 21 fret Indian rosewood fingerboard and a maple neck, which the rosewood gives the bass a luxurious look, feel and warmth while the maple balances it with some brightness. For electronics, it is equipped with 2 FCGR original Jazz Bass style pickups and preamp, and they provide the classic jazz bass sound profile while the preamp system provides more flexibility and power.

This bass is used and it is in mint condition. There are some light cleaning marks and scratches on the top of back of the body, due to normal usage. The protective plastic film on the electronics plate is still intact. The neck and fingerboard are in great condition with no scratches or dings. The frets are virtually new. The headstock is also in great condition. The chrome finish on the hardwares remain intact, The electronics are working normally and the truss rod functions in both directions. This bass comes with a certificate of authenticity and an original gig bag.


This item is listed on Reverb.com as well as being sold Bellaromeo online store and our physical showroom in Wan Chai, Hong Kong.  In rare occasions, the item may be sold out first via other channels, in which case we regrettably will need to prioritise the order fulfilment by the time stamp of the order being placed on a first-come-first-sold basis.  You may find  additional images and item specifications on Bellaromeo online store.

item.title: Fender Custom Shop Vintage Custom '51 Precison Bass 2019 [Mod/Used] — $3,888
item.description:
$3,888 · Excellent · Bella Romeo Music

In the early '50s, Leo Fender revolutionized the bass sound in modern music by creating the Precision bass, which for the enabled amplification to match volumes for the rest of the band, as compared to double basses that came before. It's also fretted so pitch can be achieved more precisely, and hence the name. This custom shop '51 Precision Bass model is based on the first version of Precision bass that came into production. When we say Precision bass, a familiar sound immediately comes to mind. But, that sound in our heads came from post 1957's 3rd version, with a split-coil P pickup. Or more likely than not, we are more accustomed to the alder body /rosewood fingerboard versions came in the early '60s. The '51 Precision sounds and plays differently. Many vintage factors contribute to the differences from your more modern P bass tone:

  • Single-coil pick up based on Telecaster guitars at the time
  • Slab ash body (2-piece)
  • 2 fiber saddle at the bridge
  • '51 U neck profile A.K.A "baseball bat"

If thin necks are considered modern as opposed to vintage, then this bass is as vintage as it can ever be. The thickness of the neck does impact tone, in our opinion. Whilst it is definitely a thick and round neck, as a 4-string we didn't find it difficult to play.

The pickup is changed to a Lindy Fralin '51 Precision handwound pickup, delivering a faithful tone of that era.  The bass is strung with LaBella Deep Talkin' flats, although the originally Fender flats are included in the hard case. The tone can be related to double bass, but played with a pick it produces the classic rock n roll tones. Sustain is shorter than post-1957 versions of P basses, but no where as short as a Hofner violin bass, for example.

Ergonomics of a slab body need some getting used to, as well as the thicker and rounder neck. It balances very well seated or strapped on shoulder, as we didn't experience any neck dive.

Cosmetically this bass is very pretty and just oozes quality from Fender C.S. It is painted with flash coat lacquer in new-old-stock (NOS), meaning no relic. Flash coat is very thin as compared with normal nitrocellulose lacquer process by Fender. The thin coat of Nocaster blonde finish is quite transparent, and we think the finish will wear quickly and beautifully.

This is a used bass, and is in Excellent+ condition. The body shows virtually no wear front, side, and back. The black pickguard has fine scratches and also visible wear at the truss rod adjustment area. The quartersawn maple neck and fingerboard is gorgeous - it is also finished in flash coat lacquer and the back of the neck has some sign of usage as the finish became duller with playing.  The electronics work flawlessly, as well as the truss rod. Hardwares have very little signs of usage; the chrome still shines. The vintage style output jack needs to be used carefully, since it is not secured by any screws. It's built that way to be authentic to its vintage style, and with care i.e. don't yank out the lead cable, you should be fine.

The bass comes with its original tweed deluxe hardshell case, the bass' original pickup and bridge plate covers with their original screws, COA and other case candies.

This item is listed on Reverb.com as well as being sold Bellaromeo online store and our physical showroom in Wan Chai, Hong Kong. You may find additional images and item specifications on Bellaromeo online store.

item.title: Rickenbacker 4001 V63 2001 [Used] — $4,134
item.description:
$4,134 · Mint · Bella Romeo Music

Rickenbacker is a USA based instrument manufacturer, and their instruments are beloved by famous acts around the world, such as the Beatles, Deep Purple and Pink Floyd, and the Rickenbacker 4001 V63 Mapleglo that we have here at Bella Romeo showcases why. The V63 replicates the model used by Paul McCartney, and is also similar by design with the version used by Chris Squire.  It features a maple body construction, which gives it a bright and snappy tone. It comes with a 20-fret rosewood fingerboard and a maple neck, which the rosewood gives a warm and rounded tonal profile and the maple neck provides a fast attack. For electronics, it is equipped with a single coil pickup in the bridge and the classic toaster single coil pick up in the neck.

This bass is used and it is in mint- condition. The top of the body and sides is in perfect condition with no noticeable nicks or scratches. There are some very minor scratches at the back of the body, visible only upon close examination. The neck and fretboard are also in perfect condition with no nicks and scratches. The frets are virtually new. There is a very minor nick on the bottom of the headstock. The chrome finish remains intact, the electronics are working normally, and the truss rod functions in both direction.

This bass comes with an original deluxe hardshell case.

item.description:
$5,989 · Very Good · Bella Romeo Music

Rickenbacker is a USA based instrument manufacturer, and their instruments are beloved by famous acts around the world, such as the Beatles, Deep Purple and Pink Floyd. Here at Bella Romeo music, we present a vintage 1973 Rickenbacker 4001 in Jetglo. This version has the checker binding around the body, but not crushed pearl inlays. 

This bass guitar has a maple body wood construction, which offers bright tonal character and crisp attack. It features a 20-fret rosewood fingerboard and 3-piece walnut/ maple neck-through neck. The headstock has the walnut wing feature which is synonymous with Rickenbacker instruments.  The rosewood gives the instrument warmth and classy aesthetics, and the 3 piece walnut/ maple neck gives a combination of tight low ends and brightness, and the neck-through feature gives the bass more sustain as well. For electronics, it is equipped with the Rickenbacker toaster single coil in the neck position and Rickenbacker single coil in the bridge position, which gives the classic Rickenbacker sound that so many musicians adored.

This bass is used and it is in good condition. Since this bass is a vintage instrument, there are visible scratches and dings all around the body.  The most visible wear is the scratch on the back of the body horn area. There are paint chips on the headstock edges. There are about 70% of the frets remaining. item.title: Nik Huber Rietbergen Bass Worn Onyx Black 2018 [Used] — $6,043
item.description:
$6,043 · Excellent · Bella Romeo Music

Nik Huber Guitars was founded by Nik Huber in 1996.  Today a team of 8 highly skilled specialists is building around 240 instruments a year, all hand-crafted. Since the first attendance at the Frankfurt Musik Messe in 1997, Nik Huber Guitars gained a worldwide reputation as a serious manufacturer of high-end electric-guitars. In 1999 the expanding company moved to a commercial park in its hometown, Rodgau – 20 km south of Frankfurt/Germany, and resides now in a fully, state-of-the-art equipped workshop.  Bella Romeo Music is very proud to be a Nik Huber authorised dealer in Hong Kong.

Rietbergen Bass

The first bass in the Nik Huber catalog comes with a single powerful P-Bass pickup, which is enough to embolden your sound at even its most profound depths. A down-to-earth bass, the Rietbergen Bass comes stripped of everything except exactly what you need – the essence of groove and rock!  For those who have it flowing through their veins (not to mention soul running through their fingers), this black pioneer stays forever true.  If you look at the back of the instrument - it is not so down-to-earth anymore; the flamed maple neck and the body back are simple stunning to look at.  

Yes, and Michael League has one of these.  The bass produces a good fat thump, with its flat wound strings installed now.  It is particularly satisfying playing with muted thumb technique, but of course it can play Motown and Beatles with finesse too.  

This bass is used and is in Excellent Condition.  It has very minimal signs of usage - although we cannot identify any significant scratch or nick.  It would be the aged hardware (i.e. dullness for the bridge and pickup cover) that shows hints of use. 


This item is listed on Reverb.com as well as being sold Bellaromeo online store and our physical showroom in Wan Chai, Hong Kong.  In rare occasions, the item may be sold out first via other channels, in which case we regrettably will need to prioritise the order fulfilment by the time stamp of the order being placed on a first-come-first-sold basis.  You may find  additional images and item specifications on Bellaromeo online store.

item.title: Le Fay Le Fay Midas 5 Set Neck Bass [Used] — $6,978
item.description:
$6,978 · Excellent · Bella Romeo Music

Le Fay has been handcrafting great-sounding instruments from selected woods for over 30 years. Also components such as pickups, saddles and bridges of Le Fay electric basses are developed and manufactured directly by Le Fay. That's why everything is perfectly coordinated and easy to play. For these reasons, professionals around the world, in some cases for decades, swear on Le Fay instruments.

Le Fay is currently no longer building the Midas, as it has probably evolved into models such as Capone or Karlsson (bridge tuning system being the main difference).

The tonal variability of this bass is unmatched, with 3 pickups that can be used in any combination, enabled by the on/off switches for each pickup.  And the mid and bridge PUs can be operated in either parallel or in series.  This custom order Le Fay has factory-installed Glockenklang pre-amp system, with bass, mid, treble cut or boost.  The pre-amp, as typical for Glockenklang, is very clear and, if desired, can be very bright.

This bass is used and is in Excellent condition.  There is feeling that it has been used with care, but no obvious nicks or scratches.  Frets remain quite tall around 80%, and they are stainless steel.  The bass comes with a generic gig bag.


This item is listed on Reverb.com as well as being sold Bellaromeo online store and our physical showroom in Wan Chai, Hong Kong.  In rare occasions, the item may be sold out first via other channels, in which case we regrettably will need to prioritise the order fulfilment by the time stamp of the order being placed on a first-come-first-sold basis.  You may find  additional images and item specifications on Bellaromeo online store.

item.description:
$4,500 · Excellent · Bella Romeo Music

Fender Custom Shop 64 Jazz Bass Relic Fretless Black

The '64 specification jazz base with maple neck and round rose plate with a round body on the alder body creates a vintage instrument-like atmosphere with a vintage instrument. Our individual is coated on the fingerboard surface when modified on the fretless specification. The pronunciation is good, and the sound is well-established, and the warm and rich middle range sounds comfortably and the sound is output.

With an alder body, maple neck, and rounded rosewood fingerboard has been given a relic treatment that looks as if it was carved naturally, without any sense of artificiality, giving it the feel of a vintage instrument.  It has good pronunciation, good grain, and a warm fretless tone with a tight and rich mid-range that resonates comfortably.  

This bass is used and is in Mint- condition.  There are no obvious signs of use, except very light string marks on the fretboard.  The previous owner doesn't really play fretless so its usage was very limited - more of a collected item to honor Jaco Pastorius.  The basss comes with its original Fender deluxe hardshell case, COA (floor traveller), and original case candies.

item.description:

G&L Tribute SB-2 Bass Guitar Features:

  • A reimagining of the classic SB-2 4-string electric bass
  • Straight from the minds of Leo Fender and George Fullerton
  • California-built PJ pickups have you covered for virtually every style of music

item.title: 【new】Xotic / XJ-1T 5st Charcoal Frost Metallic Medium Aged 4.395kg #3622【GIB Yokohama】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $4,649
item.description:
$4,649 · Brand New · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Xotic is a brand founded in LA by Japanese builder Hiro Miura.

In Japan, many famous musicians use Xotic guitars, including Yoshiki Takahashi, BJ from indigo jam unit, and Katsushi Morioka. Internationally, many famous musicians such as Chuck Rainey, Rhonda Smith, and Travis Carlton use Xotic guitars. (Titles omitted)

This is the most popular model, the XJ-1T.

The XJ series, a line manufactured in Japan, has been highly regarded, especially among studio workers.

While fundamentally built in the Fender style, several aspects have been updated with modern touches.

The body is made of alder, and the fingerboard is rosewood.

A classic combination with a rich midrange.

The finish is lacquer, and the color is Charcoal Frost Metallic, Medium Aged.

The combination of the body color and the red tortoiseshell pickguard is eye-catching.

Luminova luminous position markers are used.

The tuning machines are HIPSHOT Ultralite, and the bridge is a HIPSHOT Style B.

The pickups are Raw Vintage.

These are handmade in California and are characterized by their clear, crisp, and raw output.

They offer a more sensitive response to the player's fingertips than previous models.

The neck pickup is deeper, the bridge pickup is more three-dimensional, and the mix provides a clear and tight low end.

The preamp is an Xotic original.

Controls include neck volume (active/passive switch), bridge volume (series/parallel switch), passive tone, treble, middle, bass, middle frequency switch, and treble frequency switch.

The sound is refined, with sufficient low frequencies and pleasant harmonics.

Playability is also very high, making it a high-level instrument overall.

Especially for session and studio musicians, there is always a demand for a JB that combines modern playability and preamp features with a traditional sound.

However, there aren't many products that meet that quality standard.

I believe the Xotic XJ is one of the best solutions to meet that demand.

It will be delivered after being adjusted for easy playability by GIB's craftsmen.

Accessories: Gig bag, spec sheet, warranty card, hex wrench

item.title: Schecter Stiletto Extreme-4 Active 4-String Bass 2010s - See-Thru Black — $400
item.description:
$400 · Very Good · Celine's Gear Outlet


The Schecter Stiletto Extreme-4 is a highly rated, affordable 4-string bass Schecter Guitar known for its gorgeous aesthetics and aggressive, punchy tones. It features a fast maple neck, 24 jumbo frets, and active electronics that give musicians maximum control over their low end

Condition:

~ It has a minor scratch on the back and a small nick on the neck (shown in the last photo for full transparency).

~It’s strung with new Belladonna tape-wound strings, giving it a unique look, feel, and warm tone.

~This bass has been a dream to own, but it's time for someone else to enjoy it. Feel free to message me with any questions or if you'd like additional photos.

Key Specifications & Features

  • Body: Mahogany (or basswood) with a highly figured, quilted maple top Stiletto Extreme 4 Review | TalkBass.com
  • Neck: Maple with a comfortable, slim "C" shape Schecter Guitar Research Stiletto Extreme-4 Bass Guitar - See-Thru ...
  • Fretboard: Rosewood with Pearloid/Abalone Vector inlays Schecter Stiletto Extreme 4 Bass Guitar: Product and Offers
  • Frets: 24 X-Jumbo frets Schecter Stiletto Extreme 4 Bass Guitar: Product and Offers
  • Scale Length: Standard 34 inches Schecter Stiletto Extreme 4 Bass
  • Pickups: Dual Schecter Diamond Bass Humbuckers Schecter Stiletto Extreme-4 Bass Guitar: Product and Offers
  • Electronics: Active 2-band EQ (Bass boost/cut, Treble boost/cut) powered by a 9V battery Schecter Stiletto Extreme 4 Bass

item.title: 【new】Xotic / XJ-1T 5st Black over CAR Heavey Aged 4.300kg #3621【GIB Yokohama】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $4,735
item.description:
$4,735 · Brand New · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Xotic is a brand founded in LA by Japanese builder Hiro Miura.

In Japan, many famous musicians use Xotic guitars, including Yoshiki Takahashi, BJ from indigo jam unit, and Katsushi Morioka. Internationally, many famous musicians such as Chuck Rainey, Rhonda Smith, and Travis Carlton use Xotic guitars. (Titles omitted)

This is the most popular model, the XJ-1T.

The XJ series, manufactured in Japan, has been highly regarded, especially among studio workers.

While fundamentally built in the Fender style, several aspects have been updated with modern touches.

The body is made of alder, and the fingerboard is rosewood.

A classic combination with a rich midrange.

The finish is lacquer, with a multi-layered color of Black and Candy Apple Red, in a Heavy Aged finish.

It features heavy relicing, giving it a very distinctive look.

The position markers use Luminova, which glows in the dark.

The tuning machines are HIPSHOT Ultralite, and the bridge is a HIPSHOT Style B.

The pickups are Raw Vintage.

These are handmade in California and are characterized by their clear, crisp, and raw output.

They offer a more sensitive response to the player's fingertips than previous models.

The neck pickup is deeper, the bridge pickup is more three-dimensional, and the mix provides a clear and tight low end.

The preamp is an Xotic original.

Controls include neck volume (active/passive switch), bridge volume (series/parallel switch), passive tone, treble, middle, bass, middle frequency switch, and treble frequency switch.

The sound is refined, with sufficient low frequencies and pleasant harmonics.

Playability is also very high, making it a high-level instrument overall.

Especially for session and studio musicians, there is always a demand for a JB that combines modern playability and preamp features with a traditional sound.

However, there aren't many products that meet that quality standard.

I believe the Xotic XJ is one of the best solutions to meet that demand.

It will be delivered after being adjusted for easy playability by GIB's craftsmen.

Accessories: Gig bag, spec sheet, warranty card, hex wrench

Text: Tomoki Hayakawa

Product photos: Tei

Manufacturer's warranty included

◯GIB, TONIQ Warranty Information

●Lifetime Free Maintenance

Instruments purchased from our store are eligible for lifetime free "String Vibration Setup" (normally ¥4,400). (Reservation required)

・Please present your warranty card with your name filled in.

・Only applicable to the person who purchased the instrument at a GIB or TONIQ physical store or online shop.

・Please bear the cost of shipping and other miscellaneous expenses.

●Re-buyback Price Guarantee

If you sell your purchased item within 6 months of purchase, we guarantee the buyback price. The price is 50% for new items and 70% for used items.

●Warranty against defects

Basic Co., Ltd. provides a warranty against defects for products sold by the company. Excluding some products such as consigned items, we will repair (or refund if repair is not possible) any malfunctions that occur during normal use within a specified warranty period from the date of purchase.

The warranty period is 3 months for used products and 1 year for new products.

Please note that the following cases will incur a charge even within the warranty period:

* Malfunctions and damage caused by transportation, movement, dropping, etc.

* Replacement of consumables.

* Malfunctions and damage caused by fire, earthquake, salt damage, gas damage, or abnormal voltage.

* Malfunctions and damage caused by handling contrary to the precautions described in the instruction manual.

* Malfunctions or damage to parts repaired or modified by parties other than our company.

* If the warranty card does not have your name, model name, serial number, etc. filled in, or if the wording has been altered.

* Failure to present the warranty card, or if it is lost.

(Warranty is only valid for purchases made within Japan.)

● Up to 15% OFF with Trade-in

When you purchase a new instrument, you will receive a 10% discount on the purchase price when you use our trade-in service. (Consignment items are excluded. This discount applies only if the trade-in value is 15% or more of the purchase price.)

Furthermore, if you trade in an instrument eligible for our monthly buyback campaign, the discount rate will be 15%.

We ship with sufficient packaging.

We also ship internationally.

●Price includes VAT & Duties & Shipping Fee
Free Shipping Worldwide. All import duties and taxes are included in the price. There are no hidden fees upon delivery.

If you return a product, you will be responsible for all shipping and customs fees.

Please be sure to check the voltage and power specifications of your country before purchasing and using.

item.title: 【used】Ernie Ball Music Man / StingRay 3EQ White 2000 4.275kg #E20951【Consignment】【GIB Yokohama】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $2,493
item.description:
$2,493 · Good · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Music Man emerged as Leo Fender's second brand and became beloved by bassists worldwide.
Many artists, including Louis Johnson and Flea, are known to use their basses. (Titles omitted)

The StingRay is a classic model that enjoys overwhelming popularity within the brand.
It's no exaggeration to say that, alongside Fender's Jazz Bass and Precision Bass, it's one of the most popular basses in the world.

This particular bass was manufactured in 2000.

The body is made of ash, and the neck and fretboard are made of maple.

A beautiful, subtle flame pattern is visible on the back of the neck.

The pickups and preamp are original.

It features a 3-band EQ.

Its distinctive design features a single humbucking pickup mounted at the bridge.

The unique sound, with its powerful, punchy bass and treble, can only be achieved with this bass.

It's a legendary instrument that anyone playing funk or nuanced music should try at least once. The frets have approximately 70% remaining.
The truss rod is in good condition.
The neck shows some warping up to the 24th fret. It is playable without problems at 2.7mm string tension (4th string) and 2.2mm string tension (1st string).
The body and paintwork show a small dent of about 0.3mm on the horn. There is a scratch of about 1cm on the side of the body (jack side). There is slight discoloration due to sun exposure. The metal parts show some tarnishing.
The electronics are in good condition.
No obvious modifications were observed.
Controls: Master Volume, Treble, Middle, Bass
Nut Width: 43.0mm
Bridge String Spacing: 19.0mm
Scale Length: 34 Inch
Year of Manufacture: 2000
Country of Manufacture: USA

This guitar will be delivered after being adjusted for playability by a GIB craftsman.

This is a consignment item, so shipping costs may differ from the usual.

Accessories: ARIA PB-EB (soft case)

Text: Tomoki Hayakawa

Inspection 담당: Oshima

item.title: 【new】Sadowsky / MX21 HP5 TBT/R 4.36kg #SMX F 000794-25【GIB Hyogo】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $1,449
item.description:
$1,449 · Excellent · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Sadowsky is a high-end New York brand with top musicians like Marcus Miller and Will Lee as clients.

Previously, the Metroline Express was manufactured in Japan, but as Warwick took over the distribution of Sadowsky products, manufacturing moved to a factory in China.

While keeping the selling price down, high quality is maintained, making it a very unique product in its price range.

Initially, the Metroline Express series did not have the Sadowsky logo, but this has now been changed.

Furthermore, the neck has been changed to roasted maple.

It has a satin finish and a smooth feel.

The back panel is a screwless type, similar to Warwick.

Looking inside the cavity, you can see that it is shielded with copper foil tape. It's wonderful.

The pickups are PJ type, with a Precision Bass style in the neck position and a Jazz Bass style in the bridge position.

The bridge pickup is a hum-canceling type, minimizing the noise often associated with PJ pickups. The preamp circuit board is almost identical to that of top-of-the-line models priced around 1 million yen, which I think is a significant advantage.

The controls are volume, balance, bass, and treble.

A push-pull switch allows you to bypass the active circuit and play passively.

The powerful and crisp sound sounds a little more flamboyant than recent NYC models.

Some people might even prefer this sound based on the tone alone.

It seems that creating a product with the same high quality at the same price point is not easy.

Highly recommended.

●SPECS

5-String Electric Bass
Scale Length: 34"" (864 mm)
Bolt-On Construction
Body Wood: Okoume
Neck Wood: Roasted Maple
Fingerboard: Roasted Maple
Fingerboard Radius: 12""
Nut: Sadowsky Just-A-Nut III
Nut Width: 1.875" (47.6 mm)
Frets: 21 Nickel Silver (width: 0.094"" (2.4 mm) / height: 0.051"" (1.3 mm))
Neck Pickup: Passive Sadowsky P-Style
Bridge Pickup: Passive hum-cancelling Sadowsky stacked J-Style in 60s position
Electronics: Sadowsky Active 2-Way
Controls: Volume / Balance / Treble / Bass
Push/pull function at Bass knob for preamp Bypass
Tuners: Sadowsky Light machine heads with open gear
Security Locks: Sadowsky S-style
Bridge System: Sadowsky bridge with quick string release
Hardware Color: Chrome
Strings: 045 - 135 Stainless Steel
Included Accessories: Sadowsky Fretboard Protector and Hex Key Tools
Gig Bag: Sadowsky Portabag (SAC BAG BASS PORTA)

Accessories: Original gig bag, warranty card

Text: Shunsuke Saga

Product Photos: Lee

item.description:
$260 · Good · Noel's Shop

Cool older model Ibanez bass. It's got a push pull volume pot that gives different coil split options. It has a pretty low output that definitely benefits from boosting the bass and treble knobs. Not sure if the preamp may need rreplacement down the line. The pickups seem to be the stock Duncan designed pickups. Definitely a cool bass. I can throw in a gig bag.

item.title: Music Man Stingray 5 - gold sparkle — $2,680.28
item.description:

Sehr guter original Stingray

- Bünde gut

- Halsstab ok

Worldwide Shipping!

This Aria Pro II SB-800 is an awesome bass in great condition. A high-qualilty, Matsumoku build ca. 1990s, this neck through bass is a smooth player and handsome devil. The neck is straight with low n' quick action and the electronics are functioning as they should. Excellent tone and versatility.

Cosmetically, the bass looks great. Nicks, dings, scratches, scuffs and patina present but overall quite clean. Super hot in dark walnut finish with eye in the sky inlays. Included is a Japanese naugahyde soft case.

Free local pick up from PTD Vintage in Chicago's Wicker Park neighborhood available and encouraged. Shipping is a flat $125 in the lower 48.
